skill.name: ariadne-loop
skill.description: Write verifiable Loop Engineering specs for Codex, Claude Code, OpenClaw, and AI coding agents.
skill.summary: A documentation-only skill that turns vague agent work into a bounded loop contract with snapshot JSON, observable verifiers, stop rules, and human gates.
skill.author: zhangzeyu99-web

security_audit.summary: The skill is a 143-line markdown specification (SKILL.md only) that instructs AI agents how to author bounded Loop Engineering contracts. The static analyzer flagged 27 patterns, but every flagged line is a false positive: the 21 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' hits are markdown inline-code backticks around JSON field names and bash command examples inside fenced code blocks, the two 'Hardcoded URL' hits point to the project's own GitHub repo and GitHub Pages playground, the three 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' hits are natural-language lines with no crypto primitives, and the 'System reconnaissance' hit is a 'Verifier Rules' section header. There is no executable code, no network exfiltration, and no prompt-injection text.

content.value_statement: Coding agents often produce work that cannot be checked. This skill turns vague agent requests into a bounded loop contract with snapshot JSON, observable verifiers, stop rules, and explicit human gates.
actual_capabilities: Define a snapshot JSON with title, goal, current_state, recent_progress, constraints, verifiers, external_effects, and risk fields.
Generate an agent packet that includes an inspect-act-verify-decide cycle, concrete verifiers, stop rules, rollback behavior, human gates, and a JSON-only report contract.
Invoke the ariadne-loop CLI for quickstart, init, write, make, check, from-issue, and supervise subcommands when the CLI is installed.
Convert a GitHub issue title and body into an executable agent loop using the from-issue subcommand.
Supervise running loops by appending JSON reports to a JSONL file and producing a decision JSON that calls continue, stop, needs_human, or rollback.
Apply verifier rules that prefer observable gates (command output, screenshot readback, artifact checks, remote-state readback) and reject weak gates like 'looks good' or 'agent says it completed'.
limitations: The skill is documentation only; the ariadne-loop CLI is a separate tool that the AI agent must run, and this skill does not install or invoke it directly.
The skill cannot enforce verifier quality; humans must define the observable gates that match their codebase and release process.
The skill has no live GitHub API integration in the markdown itself; workflows that need to fetch issue bodies depend on the ariadne-loop CLI.
Actions that have real-world side effects (commit, push, release, deploy, payment) are blocked behind human gates, so the skill cannot complete those steps autonomously.

use_cases.title: Plan a Bounded Refactor
use_cases.description: A senior engineer wants a refactor that an agent can run safely overnight. The skill produces a snapshot JSON and agent packet that bound the work, define stop rules, and require human approval before push.

use_cases.title: Convert a Bug Report Into a Verified Fix
use_cases.description: A maintainer pastes a GitHub issue and the skill turns it into a loop spec whose verifiers require a failing reproduction to exist, then to pass, and a diff with no unrelated churn.

use_cases.title: Supervise an Ongoing Release Loop
use_cases.description: A release manager runs a long-running loop and accumulates JSONL reports. The skill describes how to feed those reports into the supervise subcommand to get a continue, stop, needs_human, or rollback decision before tagging the release.

prompt_templates.title: Bootstrap a Demo Loop
prompt_templates.prompt: Run ariadne-loop quickstart --output .ariadne/quickstart to create a snapshot, loop JSON, agent packet, sample reports, and a supervision decision so I can see the full contract end to end.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-run demo of the Loop Engineering workflow

prompt_templates.title: Initialize a Bugfix Loop
prompt_templates.prompt: Run ariadne-loop init --preset bugfix --output loop-snapshot.json and then ariadne-loop make --input loop-snapshot.json --output loop.json --format json so I have a bugfix loop ready for the agent.
prompt_templates.scenario: Start a bugfix loop from a preset

prompt_templates.title: Convert a GitHub Issue Into a Loop
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the issue at <URL>, save the body to issue.md, then run ariadne-loop from-issue --title "<issue title>" --body-file issue.md --output issue-loop.json and show me the resulting verifiers and stop rules.
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn a real bug report into an executable agent task

prompt_templates.title: Supervise a Running Loop
prompt_templates.prompt: The agent has been appending JSON reports to reports.jsonl for the loop defined in loop.json. Run ariadne-loop supervise --loop loop.json --reports reports.jsonl --output decision.json and summarize the decision as continue, stop, needs_human, or rollback.
prompt_templates.scenario: Make a gate decision on an in-progress loop

output_examples.input: User says: I need a refactor loop for the auth module. Plan it and produce the snapshot.
output_examples.output: A snapshot JSON object with title, goal, current_state, recent_progress, constraints, verifiers, external_effects, and risk fields, plus a matching agent packet that names the inspect-act-verify-decide cycle, the tests that must pass, the stop rule when diff size exceeds a budget, and a human gate on push.

output_examples.input: User says: Run one turn of the loop and report back.
output_examples.output: A single JSON report with action_id (inspect, act, verify, or decide), status (continue, stop, needs_human, or rollback), evidence array of what was observed this turn, next_step, passed_verifiers, and failed_verifiers.

output_examples.input: User says: We have 12 reports from the agent overnight, do we ship?
output_examples.output: A decision JSON calling continue, stop, needs_human, or rollback, citing the specific verifiers that passed or failed in the accumulated JSONL reports, and naming any human gate that still needs approval.

best_practices: Define verifiers as observable gates tied to artifacts (command output, screenshots, generated files, remote readback) rather than subjective phrases like 'looks good' or 'done'.
Require explicit human confirmation for any action that changes shared state: commit, push, tag or release, package publish, deploy, deletion, or sending external messages.
After any approved external effect, read back the real target (commit hash, deployed URL, issue state) before reporting success, and include the readback evidence in the next JSON report.
anti_patterns: Treating 'agent says it completed' or an unverified screenshot as a verifier; this defeats the bounded contract and silently accepts unverifiable work.
Skipping the snapshot JSON and jumping straight to a free-form prompt; without snapshot state and verifiers, the agent cannot self-check its own turn.
Running commit, push, release, or deploy steps without a human gate in the loop contract; this turns a verifiable loop into an autonomous one with no override point.

faq.question: What is Loop Engineering?
faq.answer: Loop Engineering is a method for turning vague agent work into a bounded contract that includes snapshot state, observable verifiers, stop rules, and human gates, so each agent turn is checkable.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown specification. It tells the AI agent how to use the ariadne-loop CLI, but the skill itself does not run any code or access the network.

faq.question: Which AI agents does it support?
faq.answer: The skill is written for Codex, Claude Code, and OpenClaw, and works with any coding agent that follows a snapshot plus packet contract and returns the JSON report format described in the skill.

faq.question: Do I need the ariadne-loop CLI installed?
faq.answer: No. You can produce the snapshot JSON and the agent packet directly in your response. The CLI is only needed for quickstart, init, write, make, check, from-issue, and supervise subcommands.

faq.question: What is a human gate?
faq.answer: A human gate is a rule that requires explicit human confirmation before an action that has real-world side effects, such as commit, push, tag, release, deploy, deletion, sending external messages, or payment.

faq.question: How is this different from a regular prompt?
faq.answer: A regular prompt asks the agent to do work. This skill structures that work as a loop with snapshot state, observable verifiers, and stop rules, so the next agent turn is verifiable instead of trusted.
